source.title: Long-Term Potentiation at C-fiber Synapses in the Anterior Cingulate Cortex Contributes to Chronic Pain

confidence_rationale: Strong mechanistic rodent evidence that sustained C-fibre (affective-pain) input drives LTP-like plasticity in ACC synapses, producing a persistent shift in ACC responsiveness that outlasts the originating nociceptive input. This is the canonical cellular mechanism by which sustained z_harm_a would write a drift into a downstream baseline; it also bears on sleep/offline normalisation by showing the plasticity has an explicit timecourse. Mapping fidelity moderate: rodent ACC LTP is a cellular mechanism, not a direct validation of writing into drive_level specifically.
failure_signatures: If the REE slow-write EMA saturates within a single episode (timescale minutes), it is mis-modelling the biology, which operates over days of sustained input.

If the ACC-mGluR5 literature shows the plasticity is partially reversed during sleep/offline states (supported by sleep-deprivation-and-pain studies) and SD-032e has no offline decay, SD-032e systematically underestimates recovery.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Sustained C-fibre (nociceptive affective) input into rodent ACC drives long-term potentiation at ACC synapses via mGluR5-dependent plasticity. This LTP is necessary for the maintenance of chronic pain phenotype and pharmacological blockade reverses both the plasticity and the behavioural sensitisation.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-032e's core architectural move — sustained z_harm_a produces a persistent shift in a downstream variable via slow-plasticity-like accumulation — has direct cellular-mechanism support in this literature. The EMA of sustained z_harm_a is a reasonable high-level abstraction of synaptic LTP at ACC. The write target in the biology is local ACC synaptic efficacy, which translates to REE as a bias/gain term that ACC applies downstream, NOT necessarily drive_level per se. Closest REE equivalent is a learned scaling factor that pACC-analog writes onto its downstream output.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Rodent-to-agent transfer is always non-trivial. The biology is a synaptic-efficacy change local to ACC, not a write into a separate interoceptive baseline module. A literal mapping would put the 'setpoint' inside pACC itself as an internal gain state, and route its downstream effect through pACC's output connections. SD-032e currently proposes writing directly into drive_level, which compresses two biological steps (ACC plasticity, then ACC-modulated influence on downstream interoceptive processing) into one. Reasonable simplification for a first substrate; the SD doc should note it.
mapping.source_context: Adult male rat ACC slice and in-vivo electrophysiology with peripheral nerve injury chronic-pain model. Behavioural sensitisation correlates with ACC LTP over 7-14 days.

confidence_components.notes: Rodent neurophysiology in a well-replicated chronic-pain paradigm. Mapping fidelity higher than the fMRI papers because the mechanism (slow plasticity from sustained affective-pain input) maps cleanly onto SD-032e's EMA abstraction. Transfer risk moderate because the biology is cellular and REE is behavioural-level, but the abstraction is clean.
